title: jacobaea erucifolia subsp. grandidentata
intro: Jacobaea erucifolia subsp. grandidentata, also known as the Jacob's Resin or Common Ragwort, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. Jacobaea erucifolia subsp. grandidentata is traditionally used to prepare herbal remedies like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
